name: Coastal1
description_short: Coastal1 Credit Union is a Rhode Island-based financial institution established in 1926, serving over 140,600 members with $3.3B in assets.
description_long:
Coastal1 Credit Union was established in 1926 and is headquartered in Pawtucket, Rhode Island. As the 2nd largest credit union in the state, it has grown to serve over 140,600 loyal members and manages more than $3.3 billion in assets. The institution operates with over 300 dedicated employees and maintains a single branch location supported by 19 ATMs across its network.

Coastal1 offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business financial products. Members can access checking and savings accounts, money market accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), IRAs/KEOGH accounts, personal loans, auto loans, business loans, and mortgage options (fixed-rate, adjustable-rate, and balloon/hybrid mortgages). Digital services include online banking, mobile app access for iOS and Android, bill pay, online loan payments, mobile payments, remote deposit capture, and e-statements. The credit union also provides financial tools such as interest rate comparison tools and financial calculators, along with community support through financial workshops and student scholarships.

Coastal1 distinguishes itself through its long operational history and significant asset base relative to Rhode Island's credit union landscape. The institution operates under a state charter and maintains a diverse membership base with substantial account diversity: over 137,700 regular savings accounts, 42,100 share draft accounts, 6,900 money market accounts, and 21,600 share certificates (CDs). Drive-thru teller service is available, alongside remote banking conveniences. Membership eligibility is tailored to Rhode Island residents.

However, recent member feedback raises service quality concerns. Available reviews reflect mixed satisfaction, with at least one detailed complaint regarding customer service responsiveness and reimbursement disputes. The institution is also currently experiencing reported service issues according to the website, with both lobby and drive-thru hours listed as currently closed at the time of this profile. Prospective members should verify current operational status and service availability before joining. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Online banking with bill pay and external account transfers
pros: Established 1926 with strong 98-year track record of stability and longevity
Second-largest credit union in Rhode Island with $3.3B in assets under management
Serves 140,600+ members with over 300 dedicated employees
Comprehensive mortgage options including fixed-rate, adjustable-rate, and balloon/hybrid mortgages
Mobile banking available on both iOS and Android platforms
Remote deposit capture and mobile payment capabilities for digital convenience
19 ATM locations across service area network
Financial education resources including workshops and student scholarships
cons: Currently experiencing service issues with website, mobile app, and online banking access
Negative member review citing poor customer service and disputed reimbursement claims
Limited branch accessibility with only 1 physical location in Pawtucket
Both lobby and drive-thru hours currently closed at main office location
Mixed member satisfaction rating of 3.0/5 stars based on available reviews
best_for: Rhode Island residents seeking full-service credit union membership with comprehensive lending options
Homebuyers interested in diverse mortgage products from a stable, established lender
Members prioritizing digital banking convenience with mobile app and remote deposit capabilities
Small business owners seeking business loans from a credit union with significant asset base
diagnosis: Coastal1 is best for Rhode Island residents seeking traditional full-service credit union banking with mortgage lending, backed by significant assets and institutional stability. However, prospective members should verify current service availability and investigate recent complaints about customer service and operational issues before opening accounts.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
